intro: Micropaegma cupulatum, also known as Cupulate Micropaegma, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in Australian folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are said to aid in wound healing and reduce the severity of cold and flu symptoms. The bioactive constituents of Micropaegma cupulatum include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Micropaegma cupulatum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate respiratory issues.
